groups.note: Set on spans wrapping VoyageEmbedding.embed() calls. Uses OTel GenAI semantic conventions for standard AI model attributes and custom cluster_whisperer.embedding.* for embedding-specific metrics. SpanKind: CLIENT (outbound HTTP calls to Voyage AI).

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.embedding.dimensions
attributes.brief: Dimensionality of the returned embedding vectors

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.embedding.input_count
attributes.brief: Number of text inputs sent for embedding

attributes.name: gen_ai.operation.name

members.brief: Chat completion operation such as [OpenAI Chat API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat)
Multimodal content generation operation such as [Gemini Generate Content](https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content)
Text completions operation such as [OpenAI Completions API (Legacy)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions)
Embeddings operation such as [OpenAI Create embeddings API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create)

attributes.brief: The name of the operation being performed.
attributes.note: If one of the predefined values applies, but specific system uses a different name it's RECOMMENDED to document it in the semantic conventions for specific GenAI system and use system-specific name in the instrumentation. If a different name is not documented, instrumentation libraries SHOULD use applicable predefined value.

attributes.name: gen_ai.request.model
attributes.brief: The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to.
attributes.note: The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to. If the model is supplied by a vendor, then the value must be the exact name of the model requested. If the model is a fine-tuned custom model, the value should have a more specific name than the base model that's been fine-tuned.

groups.brief: Attributes for HTTP server request spans
groups.note: Set on spans wrapping incoming HTTP requests via Hono middleware. Uses OTel HTTP semantic conventions for standard request/response attributes and custom cluster_whisperer.sync.* for the instance sync endpoint. SpanKind: SERVER (inbound request handling). Supports W3C Trace Context propagation from upstream services (e.g., k8s-vectordb-sync controller).

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.sync.delete_count
attributes.brief: Number of deletes in the sync payload

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.sync.upsert_count
attributes.brief: Number of upserts in the sync payload

attributes.name: http.request.method

members.brief: Any HTTP method that the instrumentation has no prior knowledge of.

attributes.brief: HTTP request method.
attributes.note:
HTTP request method value SHOULD be "known" to the instrumentation.
By default, this convention defines "known" methods as the ones listed in [RFC9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-methods)
and the PATCH method defined in [RFC5789](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5789.html).

If the HTTP request method is not known to instrumentation, it MUST set the `http.request.method` attribute to `_OTHER`.

If the HTTP instrumentation could end up converting valid HTTP request methods to `_OTHER`, then it MUST provide a way to override
the list of known HTTP methods. If this override is done via environment variable, then the environment variable MUST be named
OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_KNOWN_METHODS and support a comma-separated list of case-sensitive known HTTP methods
(this list MUST be a full override of the default known method, it is not a list of known methods in addition to the defaults).

HTTP method names are case-sensitive and `http.request.method` attribute value MUST match a known HTTP method name exactly.
Instrumentations for specific web frameworks that consider HTTP methods to be case insensitive, SHOULD populate a canonical equivalent.
Tracing instrumentations that do so, MUST also set `http.request.method_original` to the original value.

attributes.name: http.response.status_code
attributes.brief: [HTTP response status code](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6).

requirement_level.conditionally_required: If and only if one was received/sent.

attributes.name: http.route
attributes.brief: The matched route, that is, the path template in the format used by the respective server framework.

requirement_level.conditionally_required: If and only if it's available

attributes.note:
MUST NOT be populated when this is not supported by the HTTP server framework as the route attribute should have low-cardinality and the URI path can NOT substitute it.
SHOULD include the [application root](/docs/http/http-spans.md#http-server-definitions) if there is one.

attributes.name: url.path
attributes.brief: The [URI path](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.3) component
attributes.note: Sensitive content provided in `url.path` SHOULD be scrubbed when instrumentations can identify it.

groups.brief: GenAI semantic convention attributes for LLM/chat completion spans
groups.note: Attributes set on chat.anthropic spans created by OpenLLMetry's auto-instrumentation of the Anthropic SDK. gen_ai.tool.definitions describes which tools were available during each LLM reasoning step. See PRD #23 M3 and https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.definitions
attributes.brief: JSON array of tool definitions available to the LLM
attributes.note: Not yet in OTel semconv v1.37.0 registry. Datadog maps this to meta.tool_definitions in LLM Observability. Contains tool names, descriptions, and parameter schemas.

groups.brief: MCP tool execution attributes with GenAI semantic convention references

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.mcp.tool.name
attributes.brief: The MCP tool name
attributes.note: OTel has MCP semantic conventions (development stability) as of 2025. See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/mcp/ This custom attribute provides MCP-specific tool identification.

attributes.name: gen_ai.operation.name

members.brief: Chat completion operation such as [OpenAI Chat API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat)
Multimodal content generation operation such as [Gemini Generate Content](https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content)
Text completions operation such as [OpenAI Completions API (Legacy)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions)
Embeddings operation such as [OpenAI Create embeddings API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create)

attributes.brief: The name of the operation being performed.
attributes.note: If one of the predefined values applies, but specific system uses a different name it's RECOMMENDED to document it in the semantic conventions for specific GenAI system and use system-specific name in the instrumentation. If a different name is not documented, instrumentation libraries SHOULD use applicable predefined value.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.call.id
attributes.brief: The tool call identifier.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.name
attributes.brief: Name of the tool utilized by the agent.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.type
attributes.brief: Type of the tool utilized by the agent
attributes.note:
Extension: A tool executed on the agent-side to directly call external APIs, bridging the gap between the agent and real-world systems.
  Agent-side operations involve actions that are performed by the agent on the server or within the agent's controlled environment.
Function: A tool executed on the client-side, where the agent generates parameters for a predefined function, and the client executes the logic.
  Client-side operations are actions taken on the user's end or within the client application.
Datastore: A tool used by the agent to access and query structured or unstructured external data for retrieval-augmented tasks or knowledge updates.

groups.note: These are vendor conventions from Traceloop, not official OTel semconvs

attributes.name: traceloop.entity.input
attributes.brief: Input content (content-gated)
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true

attributes.name: traceloop.entity.name
attributes.brief: Entity identifier within the span kind

attributes.name: traceloop.entity.output
attributes.brief: Output content (content-gated)
examples: The pod 'nginx-broken' is failing due to ImagePullBackOff...
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true

attributes.name: traceloop.span.kind
attributes.brief: OpenLLMetry span category

groups.brief: Attributes for sync pipeline orchestration spans
groups.note: Set on parent pipeline spans and child stage spans for syncCapabilities() and syncInstances(). No OTel semconv exists for data pipeline orchestration; uses custom cluster_whisperer.pipeline.* namespace. SpanKind: INTERNAL (local orchestration, no network boundary).

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.deleted_count
attributes.brief: Number of stale documents deleted

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.discovered_count
attributes.brief: Number of resources discovered

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.dry_run
attributes.brief: Whether the pipeline ran in dry-run mode (skip storage)

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.inferred_count
attributes.brief: Number of capabilities inferred by LLM

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.name
attributes.brief: Pipeline identifier

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.stage
attributes.brief: Current pipeline stage

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.pipeline.stored_count
attributes.brief: Number of documents stored

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.invocation.mode

members.brief: Invoked via MCP server (e.g., from Claude Code)

attributes.brief: How the agent was invoked
attributes.note: No OTel semconv exists for invocation mode. The closest analogy is faas.trigger (serverless trigger type) but that's scoped to FaaS. This custom attribute follows OTel convention of namespacing vendor-specific attributes under the service name.

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.service.operation
attributes.brief: The high-level operation type

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.user.question
attributes.brief: User's natural language question (content-gated)
examples: Find the broken pod and tell me why it's failing
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. CLI mode only.

attributes.name: gen_ai.input.messages
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified input messages in OTel v1.37+ parts format (content-gated)
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. Manual workaround for OpenLLMetry-JS using deprecated gen_ai.prompt.N format. Format: [{"role":"user","parts":[{"type":"text","content":"..."}]}] See PRD #21 and https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

attributes.name: gen_ai.output.messages
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified output messages in OTel v1.37+ parts format (content-gated)
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. Manual workaround for OpenLLMetry-JS using deprecated gen_ai.completion.N format. Format: [{"role":"assistant","parts":[{"type":"text","content":"..."}],"finish_reason":"end_turn"}] See PRD #21 and https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

groups.brief: kubectl subprocess execution attributes with Process semantic convention references

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.k8s.namespace
attributes.brief: Kubernetes namespace from -n flag
attributes.note: Extracted from command args when -n flag is present

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.k8s.output_size_bytes
attributes.brief: Byte length of kubectl stdout
attributes.note: Only set on successful execution. Useful for debugging large responses.

attributes.name: error.type

members.brief: A fallback error value to be used when the instrumentation doesn't define a custom value.

attributes.brief: The full name of exception type.

requirement_level.conditionally_required: if and only if an error has occurred.

attributes.name: process.command_args
attributes.brief: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) as received by the process. On Linux-based systems (and some other Unixoid systems supporting procfs), can be set according to the list of null-delimited strings extracted from `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. For libc-based executables, this would be the full argv vector passed to `main`. SHOULD NOT be collected by default unless there is sanitization that excludes sensitive data.

attributes.name: process.executable.name
attributes.brief: The name of the process executable. On Linux based systems, this SHOULD be set to the base name of the target of `/proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, this SHOULD be set to the base name of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

attributes.name: process.exit.code
attributes.brief: The exit code of the process.

groups.brief: GenAI semantic convention attributes for agentic tool execution spans
groups.note: Set by withToolTracing() on spans created by OpenLLMetry's withTool(). Both gen_ai.* (OTel GenAI semconv) and traceloop.* (OpenLLMetry) attributes coexist on the same span for ecosystem compatibility. See PRD #23 and https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

attributes.name: gen_ai.operation.name

members.brief: Chat completion operation such as [OpenAI Chat API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat)
Multimodal content generation operation such as [Gemini Generate Content](https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content)
Text completions operation such as [OpenAI Completions API (Legacy)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions)
Embeddings operation such as [OpenAI Create embeddings API](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create)

attributes.brief: The name of the operation being performed.
attributes.note: If one of the predefined values applies, but specific system uses a different name it's RECOMMENDED to document it in the semantic conventions for specific GenAI system and use system-specific name in the instrumentation. If a different name is not documented, instrumentation libraries SHOULD use applicable predefined value.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.call.arguments
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified tool input arguments (content-gated)
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. Not yet a standalone attribute in OTel semconv v1.37.0 registry, but part of the execute_tool span spec. See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.call.id
attributes.brief: The tool call identifier.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.call.result
attributes.brief: Tool execution result as string (content-gated)
examples:
NAME  READY  STATUS  RESTARTS  AGE
nginx  1/1  Running  0  5m
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. Not yet a standalone attribute in OTel semconv v1.37.0 registry, but part of the execute_tool span spec. See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.description
attributes.brief: The tool description.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.name
attributes.brief: Name of the tool utilized by the agent.

attributes.name: gen_ai.tool.type
attributes.brief: Type of the tool utilized by the agent
attributes.note:
Extension: A tool executed on the agent-side to directly call external APIs, bridging the gap between the agent and real-world systems.
  Agent-side operations involve actions that are performed by the agent on the server or within the agent's controlled environment.
Function: A tool executed on the client-side, where the agent generates parameters for a predefined function, and the client executes the logic.
  Client-side operations are actions taken on the user's end or within the client application.
Datastore: A tool used by the agent to access and query structured or unstructured external data for retrieval-augmented tasks or knowledge updates.

groups.brief: Attributes for vector database operation spans
groups.note: Set on spans wrapping ChromaBackend method calls (initialize, store, search, keywordSearch, delete). Uses OTel DB semantic conventions for standard database attributes and custom cluster_whisperer.vectorstore.* for domain-specific metrics. SpanKind: CLIENT (outbound network calls to ChromaDB).

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.vectorstore.document_count
attributes.brief: Number of documents in the operation (store batch size, delete batch size)

attributes.name: cluster_whisperer.vectorstore.result_count
attributes.brief: Number of results returned from a search query

attributes.name: db.collection.name
attributes.brief: Cosmos DB container name.
attributes.note:
It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application
without attempting to do any case normalization.

The collection name SHOULD NOT be extracted from `db.query.text`,
when the database system supports query text with multiple collections
in non-batch operations.

For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same
collection name then that collection name SHOULD be used.

attributes.name: db.operation.name
attributes.brief: The name of the operation or command being executed.
attributes.note:
It is RECOMMENDED to capture the value as provided by the application
without attempting to do any case normalization.

The operation name SHOULD NOT be extracted from `db.query.text`,
when the database system supports query text with multiple operations
in non-batch operations.

If spaces can occur in the operation name, multiple consecutive spaces
SHOULD be normalized to a single space.

For batch operations, if the individual operations are known to have the same operation name
then that operation name SHOULD be used prepended by `BATCH `,
otherwise `db.operation.name` SHOULD be `BATCH` or some other database
system specific term if more applicable.

attributes.name: db.system

members.brief: Some other SQL database. Fallback only. See notes.
Deprecated, no replacement at this time.
Deprecated, Microsoft SQL Server Compact is discontinued.

attributes.brief: Deprecated, use `db.system.name` instead.

groups.brief: Attributes for Vercel AI SDK streamText LLM spans
groups.note: The Vercel AI SDK produces two span layers for each streamText call: an outer ai.streamText span (full multi-step invocation) and inner ai.streamText.doStream spans (per-step LLM API calls). gen_ai.* semantic convention attributes appear ONLY on the inner doStream spans. The outer span carries SDK-specific ai.* attributes for aggregated usage. Span events ai.stream.firstChunk and ai.stream.finish mark streaming lifecycle milestones on the inner doStream spans.

attributes.name: ai.model.id
attributes.brief: Model identifier on the outer ai.streamText span

attributes.name: ai.model.provider
attributes.brief: Provider identifier on the outer ai.streamText span

attributes.name: ai.prompt.messages
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified prompt messages sent to the LLM (content-gated)
attributes.note: Only captured when OTEL_CAPTURE_AI_PAYLOADS=true. On inner doStream spans.

attributes.name: ai.prompt.tools
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified tool definitions available to the LLM
attributes.note: On inner doStream spans. Contains tool names, descriptions, and input schemas.

attributes.name: ai.response.msToFinish
attributes.brief: Milliseconds from request start to stream completion
attributes.note: On inner doStream spans. Measures total LLM call duration.

attributes.name: ai.response.msToFirstChunk
attributes.brief: Milliseconds from request start to first response chunk
attributes.note: On inner doStream spans. Measures time-to-first-token latency.

attributes.name: ai.telemetry.functionId
attributes.brief: Logical function identifier for the telemetry-enabled call
attributes.note: Set via experimental_telemetry.functionId in the streamText config.

attributes.name: ai.telemetry.metadata.agent
attributes.brief: Custom metadata identifying the agent framework
attributes.note: Set via experimental_telemetry.metadata in the streamText config.

attributes.name: ai.usage.completionTokens
attributes.brief: Aggregated completion token count across all steps (outer span)
attributes.note: SDK-specific aggregation. Per-step counts are on doStream spans as gen_ai.usage.output_tokens.

attributes.name: ai.usage.promptTokens
attributes.brief: Aggregated prompt token count across all steps (outer span)
attributes.note: SDK-specific aggregation. Per-step counts are on doStream spans as gen_ai.usage.input_tokens.

attributes.name: gen_ai.request.model
attributes.brief: The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to.
attributes.note: The name of the GenAI model a request is being made to. If the model is supplied by a vendor, then the value must be the exact name of the model requested. If the model is a fine-tuned custom model, the value should have a more specific name than the base model that's been fine-tuned.

attributes.name: gen_ai.response.finish_reasons
attributes.brief: Array of reasons the model stopped generating tokens, corresponding to each generation received.

attributes.name: gen_ai.response.id
attributes.brief: The unique identifier for the completion.

attributes.name: gen_ai.response.model
attributes.brief: The name of the model that generated the response.
attributes.note: If available. The name of the GenAI model that provided the response. If the model is supplied by a vendor, then the value must be the exact name of the model actually used. If the model is a fine-tuned custom model, the value should have a more specific name than the base model that's been fine-tuned.

attributes.name: gen_ai.system

members.note: May be used when specific backend is unknown. May use common attributes prefixed with 'gcp.gen_ai.'.
This refers to the 'aiplatform.googleapis.com' endpoint. May use common attributes prefixed with 'gcp.gen_ai.'.
This refers to the 'generativelanguage.googleapis.com' endpoint. Also known as the AI Studio API. May use common attributes prefixed with 'gcp.gen_ai.'.

attributes.brief: Deprecated, use `gen_ai.provider.name` instead.

attributes.name: gen_ai.usage.input_tokens
attributes.brief: The number of tokens used in the GenAI input (prompt).

attributes.name: gen_ai.usage.output_tokens
attributes.brief: The number of tokens used in the GenAI response (completion).

groups.display_name: Vercel AI SDK LLM Span Attributes

groups.brief: Attributes for Vercel AI SDK ai.toolCall spans
groups.note: The Vercel AI SDK creates ai.toolCall spans for each tool invocation during a streamText agent loop. These are separate from our withToolTracing() spans (<toolName>.tool) which carry cluster_whisperer.* attributes. Both span types appear in traces and serve different observability purposes: SDK spans show the framework's view of tool execution; our spans show domain-specific context.

attributes.name: ai.toolCall.args
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified tool input arguments

attributes.name: ai.toolCall.id
attributes.brief: Unique identifier for this tool call instance

attributes.name: ai.toolCall.name
attributes.brief: Name of the tool being called

attributes.name: ai.toolCall.result
attributes.brief: JSON-stringified tool execution result
examples:
NAME  READY  STATUS  RESTARTS  AGE
nginx  1/1  Running  0  5m

groups.display_name: Vercel AI SDK Tool Call Attributes
